name: debate-review
description: Structured adversarial review with presenter, challenger, and synthesizer roles. Catches issues that single-pass review misses through cross-questioning.

teammates.description: Presents the implementation, explains design decisions, and defends architectural choices
teammates.instructions: You are the PRESENTER in a debate-review. Present the implementation clearly, explain your design decisions, and respond to challenges from the challenger. Focus on correctness, maintainability, and alignment with requirements.

teammates.description: Cross-examines the implementation, identifies weaknesses, edge cases, and potential issues
teammates.instructions: You are the CHALLENGER in a debate-review. Critically examine the presenter's implementation. Identify bugs, edge cases, security issues, performance problems, and missing test coverage. Ask pointed questions. Be constructive but thorough.

teammates.description: Synthesizes debate findings into actionable recommendations and final verdict
teammates.instructions: You are the SYNTHESIZER in a debate-review. After the presenter and challenger have debated, synthesize their points into a final assessment. Identify which challenger concerns are valid, which are not, and produce a prioritized list of required changes vs nice-to-haves. Your verdict determines if the implementation passes review.

phases.name: presentation
phases.description: Presenter explains the implementation and design decisions

phases.name: synthesis
phases.description: Synthesizer produces final assessment and actionable recommendations
